macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Trump Ends Iran Deal After Renewed Strikes on Hormuz Vessels
items.summary: President Donald Trump declared the deal with Iran 'is over' following new US military strikes against Iran and the revocation of a key sanctions waiver, after Iran attacked commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: China Faces Systemic Pressures: Climate Disasters and Economic Bubble Risks
items.summary: China is buckling under severe systemic pressures including devastating flooding in Guangxi, a tornado in Hubei, and fears that an economic bubble may be about to burst alongside AI failures.

items.title: US-Iran War: Eerie Calm Broken by New Attacks on Hormuz Vessels
items.summary: The US-Iran conflict escalated with new attacks by Iran on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, breaking a fragile cease-fire and prompting a major US military response.

items.title: European Parliament Praised Ukraine's EU Accession Progress Amid Political Spat
items.summary: The European Parliament adopted a resolution praising Ukraine's progress toward EU accession while expressing regret over a contentious historical spat with Poland, emphasizing continued support for Kyiv.

items.title: China Sentences Former Official to Death in Anti-Corruption Campaign
items.summary: A Chinese court sentenced former senior official Yang Yolin to death for accepting over 2.21 billion yen ($325 million) in bribes, highlighting the severity of Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $62K Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Breakdown and Oil Price Surge
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 5.5% over 24 hours, briefly dropping below $62,000 after President Trump declared the US-Iran interim accord 'over,' triggering US airstrikes on Iran and a 2% rise in oil prices that pushed investors out of risk assets.

items.title: Zcash (ZEC) Surges 12% Above $500 Following Security Upgrade Near Proof Against Counterfeiting Flaw
items.summary: Zcash jumped 12% to top $500 as the privacy-focused protocol nears proof against a counterfeiting flaw, signaling a major security upgrade, while Monero (XMR) also gained 2% amid broader market declines.

items.title: EU Lawmakers Adopt Position Paper to Tighten Crypto Regulation Post-MiCA Implementation
items.summary: European Union lawmakers adopted a position paper calling for explicit regulatory inclusion of DeFi, crypto lending, staking, and NFTs following the end of MiCA's transitional period on July 1, requiring all crypto service providers to obtain EU-wide authorization.

items.title: Coinbase Bitcoin Premium Hits Record Negative Streak of 50 Days, Signaling US Institutional Selling Pressure
items.summary: The Coinbase Bitcoin Premium Index remained below zero for 50 consecutive days—the longest negative streak since inception—with a latest reading of -0.0742%, indicating sustained selling pressure from US institutional investors despite recent ETF inflows.

items.title: Over $500M in Leveraged Positions Wiped Out in 24 Hours as Bitcoin Pulls Back from $64,500 High
items.summary: More than $500 million in leveraged positions were liquidated within 24 hours as Bitcoin retreated from a two-week high near $64,500, driven by weak spot demand and a negative Coinbase premium, though spot ETF inflows of $223M on July 2 helped reverse an 10-day outflow streak.

items.title: Japan Approves Crypto Bill with 20% Tax Rate for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP
items.summary: Japan approved a new crypto bill establishing a 20% tax rate for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP, while SBI VC Trade hit 2 million registered accounts as Japanese institutions increasingly turn to Bitcoin and XRP.

items.title: Coinbase Wins UK License to Offer Stocks and Derivatives Alongside Crypto in Major Service Expansion
items.summary: Coinbase announced its biggest-ever service expansion in the UK, winning a license to offer stocks and derivatives alongside crypto, marking a significant step in its global regulatory footprint.

items.title: Secret Network Proposes SCRT Migration to Arbitrum with September 1 Snapshot
items.summary: Secret Network proposed migrating its SCRT token to Arbitrum, setting a September 1 snapshot date, while Ondo debuted a perps platform with 20x leverage on tokenized stock collateral.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Set to Publicly Release GPT-5.6 After US Government Approval
items.summary: OpenAI announced its most powerful AI model series, GPT-5.6 (including Sol, Terra, and Luna tiers), will launch publicly on Thursday following technical testing and meetings with the Trump administration, though the White House claims the voluntary submission was not an official approval.

items.title: Anthropic Restores Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After US Restrictions Lifted
items.summary: Anthropic confirmed it is restoring access to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after Washington removed restrictions on their release locations, while researchers revealed a 'global workspace' inside Claude enabling silent thinking.

items.title: Deep Learning Model Predicts Inpatient Hypoglycemia 24 Hours Early
items.summary: A new deep learning model that continuously analyzes electronic health record data can predict inpatient hypoglycemia up to 24 hours before it occurs, marking a significant breakthrough in clinical AI for patient safety.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Target $1 Trillion IPOs Amid AI Arms Race
items.summary: Both OpenAI and Anthropic have filed confidential IPO documents with US regulators, targeting public listings at valuations approaching $1 trillion, raising commercial stakes in the competition between the two AI giants.

items.title: GPT-5.6 Terra Priced at Half Cost of Predecessor to Compete with Anthropic and Google
items.summary: OpenAI stated that its midrange GPT-5.6 Terra model will be priced at half the cost of GPT-5.5 upon broad availability, aiming to lock in customers amid fierce competition from Anthropic and Google.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pearl Abyss Confirms Crimson Desert Development for Switch 2
items.summary: Pearl Abyss CEO Jin Young stated the company is showcasing Crimson Desert on multiple platforms, with a Switch 2 port now confirmed in development, reversing earlier skepticism about the impossible port.
items.source: Sony Gets Crushed Online And An Impossible Switch 2 Port Is Back

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Fully Playable Offline on Steam Deck
items.summary: Assassin's Creed Black Flag has been resynced for Steam Deck, allowing the entire main campaign to run offline after a single online installation, with performance verified across low, medium, and high settings.
items.source: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced on Steam Deck

items.title: PlayStation Announces Shift Away from Physical Discs Amid Fan Revolt
items.summary: Despite significant fan opposition, PlayStation is moving to eliminate physical disc support, a decision linked to broader industry trends including the delisting of video games and the decline of single-player disc-based titles.
items.source: Why PlayStation Is Killing Discs (Despite The Fan Revolt)

items.title: New Spyro Game Announced by Toys for Bob
items.summary: A new Spyro game was officially announced, developed by Toys for Bob, generating excitement among fans of the classic franchise.
items.source: Sony Gets Crushed Online And An Impossible Switch 2 Port Is Back

items.title: Lego Pokémon Sets Leaked and Confirmed
items.summary: Leaked images of upcoming Lego Pokémon sets have been officially confirmed, signaling new merchandise collaborations between Lego and Pokémon.
items.source: Sony Gets Crushed Online And An Impossible Switch 2 Port Is Back

items.title: Xbox Integrating Minecraft Deeply with Helix System
items.summary: Xbox is aggressively innovating to cut costs for Helix, with a key strategy involving deeper integration of Minecraft into the Xbox Helix ecosystem.
items.source: Sony Gets Crushed Online And An Impossible Switch 2 Port Is Back

items.title: Orbitals Previews Go Live, Launching September 3
items.summary: Previews for the upcoming game Orbitals have gone live, with the full release scheduled for September 3, 2026.
items.source: Sony Gets Crushed Online And An Impossible Switch 2 Port Is Back

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy announced $405 million in funding Wednesday to accelerate the development of its long-awaited 100-hour battery, a lithium-free alternative for grid storage.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Platform Returns to Brazil After Suspension
items.summary: The social media platform X is back in Brazil after being inaccessible for over a month, having complied with all judicial demands following a clash between Elon Musk and Brazil's highest court.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has introduced a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding its service options in the region amid growing competition in the music streaming market.

items.title: Samsung Confirms Galaxy Unpacked Event for July with Z Fold 8, Flip 8, and Watch 9
items.summary: Samsung has officially confirmed its Galaxy Unpacked event for July, with the Z Fold 8, Flip 8, and Watch 9 all expected to be unveiled in London.

items.title: Google's Pixel 11 Event Set for August 12
items.summary: Google has scheduled its Pixel 11 launch event for August 12, just one month before Apple is expected to debut its foldable iPhone.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Vulnerabilities
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on the nation's largest regulated water utility has renewed focus on the urgent need to protect critical infrastructure from digital threats.

items.title: Northvolt Head of Main Plant to Step Down
items.summary: Battery maker Northvolt announced that the head of its main plant will step down, marking a leadership change at the company amid production challenges.

items.title: Encryption Startup Keyfactor Raises Over $1B in Funding
items.summary: Encryption management startup Keyfactor has secured more than $1 billion in funding, signaling strong investor confidence in cybersecurity solutions.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth as Investors Bet on Capex-Intensive Ventures
items.summary: A new report indicates that space startups are experiencing significant funding growth as investors increasingly bet on capital-intensive ventures in the sector.

items.title: Online Shopping Returns to Growth in Europe Amid Fierce Competition
items.summary: Online shopping in Europe has returned to growth despite fierce competition among retailers, reflecting shifting consumer behaviors in the digital marketplace.
